reasoning: The current IORB rate as of May 11, 2025 is 4.40%. The recent May 6-7 FOMC meeting concluded with no inter-meeting hikes anticipated. The next FOMC meetings are scheduled for June 17-18, July 29-30, Sept 16-17, and Oct 28-29. For the first two resolution dates (May 18 and June 10), there are no scheduled FOMC meetings, reducing the chance of an increase near zero (allowing just 5% for emergency actions or technical adjustments). For the later dates (Aug 9 and Nov 7), while additional meetings will occur, market expectations overwhelmingly favor rate cuts or holds rather than hikes in 2025, although uncertainty increases with time. Thus, the probability of an increase by August is around 10% and escalates to 15% by November just in case inflation re-accelerates unexpectedly and prompts the FOMC to resume a hiking cycle.
